title: service-accounts — Artisan commands
description: Nine commands cover the machine-credential lifecycle from CLI. Every mutating command respects `service-accounts.commands.require_reason` (default on in staging + prod) which forces `--reason=<text>` capture on the audit row. Every command runs authenticated on the platform_admin guard (via an operator's PAT), never anonymously.

commands.purpose: Provision a new SA. `application` is the Application id or slug (required); `name` is the human-readable identifier (unique per application). `--tenant` optionally scopes the SA to a tenant (must belong to the same Application — refused otherwise). `--kid` optionally names an existing signer kid from `auth::jwt_signing_keys`; omitted = provisioner generates a fresh kid via the auth module. `--ttl` accepts `<n>d` (days) or ISO-8601 duration (e.g. `P90D`), default `90d`. Returns the plaintext exchange secret ONCE — store it immediately (Doppler recommended). Fires `ServiceAccountCreated` on success. Requires `--reason` in staging + prod.
usage_examples: php artisan service-account:create app_sports webhook-signer-braintree --tenant=tnt_01H... --ttl=180d --reason='Braintree webhook signer for Acme Corp'
php artisan service-account:create app_marketplace observability-collector --ttl=P365D --reason='Fleet-wide observability collector; long-TTL exempt from 90d cadence'

commands.purpose: List SAs matching the filters. Redacted — no secret material appears in CLI output. Every row includes id, name, application_slug, tenant_slug (or NULL), status, expires_at, days_until_expiry, last_used_at, kid.

Print full detail (redacted — no secret material). Includes rotation history summary (last 5 rotations with timestamp + operator), 30-day usage summary, IP-tuple fingerprint diversity, current lifecycle state. Secret hash never displayed.

Generate a fresh secret, bcrypt-hash it, write to `secret_hash`, update `secret_rotated_at` + `expires_at`. `--ttl` overrides the module-default (`service-accounts.rotation.default_secret_ttl_days`). Returns the plaintext secret ONCE. Fires `ServiceAccountSecretRotated`. Always requires `--reason` in staging + prod. Does NOT rotate `signer_kid` — use `service-account:describe` to inspect the kid + coordinate with `identity/auth` for a kid rotation event.
usage_examples: php artisan service-account:rotate svc_01H... --ttl=30d --reason='Incident response — shorten TTL until root cause is confirmed'

commands.purpose: Set `is_enabled=false`. Existing JWTs signed for this SA remain valid until natural expiry (default 300s) — to hard-revoke, use `service-account:rotate` + burn the previous kid via the auth module. Fires `ServiceAccountDisabled`. Requires `--reason`.
usage_examples: php artisan service-account:disable svc_01H... --reason='Support ticket SEC-042 — suspected compromise pending investigation'

commands.purpose: Set `is_enabled=true`. Refuses when `expires_at < now()` — caller must rotate first. Fires `ServiceAccountEnabled`. Requires `--reason`.

Soft-delete + disable. Starts the retention hold clock (default 90d after `deleted_at`). Actual hard-delete via `PurgeExpiredServiceAccountsJob`. `--confirm` required in production. Fires `ServiceAccountDeleted`.

Issue a test JWT for troubleshooting. `--aud` overrides the SA's default audience(s). `--ttl` overrides the JWT's TTL (bounded by `service-accounts.jwt.min_ttl_seconds` + `max_ttl_seconds`). `--decode` prints the decoded header + payload alongside the encoded token so the operator can verify claims. Fires `ServiceAccountJwtIssued` with a `trigger=test` label so metrics don't count test issues alongside production traffic.

Emit a usage report for one SA over the window (default 30d, accepts `24h` / `7d` / `30d` / `90d` OR ISO-8601 duration). Includes request-count histogram, unique IP /24 prefixes seen, unique user-agent hashes seen, `jti` reuse detections, average+p95 request duration, distinct destinations (audiences), and the last 100 audit-log entries. `--format` supports json (default), csv, pdf (for compliance filing). `--output` writes to a file path; without `--output`, streams to stdout. Every invocation writes its own audit row so 'who ran a report on which SA' is auditable.
